patch.value:
## Problem
When a command fails due to a missing boolean flag, the recovery bundle's suggested_retry appends a "<value>" placeholder to the flag. Boolean flags take no value, so running the suggested command verbatim fails with a different error (too many arguments). Agents are the primary consumers of suggested_retry and copy it verbatim — an executable-looking suggestion that errors when followed burns a round-trip and erodes trust in the recovery bundle.

## Repro (pm 2026.7.10)
pm close <id> done on an already-terminal item -> missing: --force -> suggested_retry: pm close <id> done --force "<value>" -> running it: too many arguments for 'close'.

## Fix direction
The suggested_retry renderer must consult each flag's arity from the flag contract: arity-0 flags rendered bare, value flags with a typed placeholder. Related territory: pm-gdi7 owns unknown-option suggestion polish in the same envelope layer; this is the retry-renderer half.

## Philosophy tie-in
project management = context management — recovery bundles are agent context; they must be executable as-is.

## Source
https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/500
GH-500: suggested_retry renders boolean flags with a "<value>" placeholder — literal suggestion fails

suggested_retry renders boolean (arity-0) flags bare (--force) and only value-taking flags with a placeholder; repro from GH-500 (pm close <id> done twice) yields a suggestion that succeeds verbatim; regression test covers boolean and value flags in suggested_retry; GH-500 closed with a comment linking the fix

author: maintainer-agent
message: Link to pm-gdi7: same recovery-envelope layer; gdi7 owns unknown-option suggestions, this owns retry-renderer arity

author: codex-root
message: Start broad atomic work-selection and agent recovery bundle; duplicate-safe all-status orientation complete

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Implementation start: active in the broad atomic work-selection and agent recovery PR. Live all-status duplicate check, full item metadata, closed prerequisite lineage, GitHub issues, security alerts, dependency audit, release state, and Sentry issue surface were reviewed before claiming. This item shares CLI/SDK contracts, recovery envelopes, and end-to-end verification with the seven sibling items in the bundle.

patch.value: Derived recovery placeholders from Commander option arity so boolean flags remain literal.
suggested_retry commands are directly runnable for boolean options.
Recovery suggestions now render --force without a bogus value placeholder, including commands preceded by global option values.
